content.text:
[Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/2). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]

You are the L1 dispatcher subagent. Your job is to spawn FIVE L2 subagents (one per language) and aggregate their results. Do NOT count lines yourself — you MUST delegate via sessions_spawn.

STEP 1: Check whether you have the sessions_spawn tool. Report clearly whether you have it or not (the config maxSpawnDepth was just raised to 2, so you may now have it).

STEP 2 (only if you have sessions_spawn): The workspace has LRU cache implementations under /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/<lang>/ for five languages: rust, go, cpp, python, java. Spawn exactly FIVE L2 subagents using sessions_spawn with mode="run", one per language, taskName like l2_<lang>. Each L2 task: "Count total lines of code in /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/lru/<lang>/ using exec (find source/test/benchmark code files, run wc -l). Report per-file counts and total. Exclude RESULT.md and markdown."

STEP 3: After spawning all five, call sessions_yield to wait. Once all five complete, aggregate into a table (language, total lines) with grand total, and return as final output. Explicitly confirm depth-2 nesting worked (that you, an L1 subagent, spawned the L2 subagents yourself).

If you do NOT have sessions_spawn, report that clearly and stop.

Begin. Execute the assigned task to completion.
I have the `sessions_spawn` tool available. Spawning five L2 subagents now.
